senses.definition: A term used in biology to refer to an underlying, foundational base related to protero- (early) or prehistoric context; not a commonly used standard vocabulary.
examples: The researcher discussed the proterobase as a primitive reference point in the lineage.
In the diagram, the proterobase marks the earliest stage analyzed.

senses.definition: An early or primitive glyph or inscription; a term sometimes used in archaeology or linguistics.
examples: The tablet bears a proteroglyph that resembles early pictographic writing.
Scholars debated whether the symbol is a proteroglyph or a later modification.

senses.definition: An extinct or archaic plural form related to proteroglyphs; used in scholarly contexts to discuss early glyphs.
examples: The corpus includes several proteroglypha from the Neolithic site.
Experts compare proteroglypha with later scripts to trace evolution.

senses.definition: Relating to early or primitive glyphs; describing inscriptions or symbols from an ancient period.
examples: The proteroglyphic inscriptions reveal early symbolic communication.
Researchers studied proteroglyphic patterns to understand prehistoric art.

senses.definition: Describing a type of something with early, glyph-like features; often used in a specialized, scholarly sense.
examples: The proteroglyphous design hints at ancient origin.
Proteroglyphous elements appear in the motif catalog.

senses.definition: A term referring to early or primitive forms of female-specific traits or roles in a fictional or scholarly context; extremely rare and may be encountered only in specialized texts.
examples: The manuscript discusses proterogynies in a mythological framework.
Few scholars study proterogynies as cultural artifacts.

senses.definition: Describing an organism in which the first sex is female; used in biology and evolutionary studies.
examples: The species is proterogynous, changing from female to male later in life.
Proterogynous patterns have implications for mating strategies.

senses.definition: The condition or pattern of an organism having female sex as the first sex; used in biological contexts.
examples: Proterogyny is observed in some fish species.
Researchers noted proterogyny as part of the life cycle.

senses.definition: In speculative or historical contexts, a hypothesis about primitive or early origins; not a standard term in mainstream science.
examples: The paper proposes a proterothesis to explain unusual glyphs.
Critics questioned the validity of the proterothesis.

senses.definition: A specimen or example that serves as the original or earliest form of a species or concept; used in biology and linguistics.
examples: The fossil is considered a proteroType for the genus.
This specimen acts as a proteroType in the study.

senses.definition: Relating to the Proterozoic eon, a geologic time period billions of years ago; used in geology and earth science.
examples: The rocks date from the Proterozoic era.
Proterozoic formations show ancient life traces.

senses.definition: A rare or archaic noun form; may appear in historical or specialized texts with limited usage.
examples: The manuscript uses the term proterve in a poetic sense.
Scholars noted the unusual proterve in the excerpt.

senses.definition: Rare plural form possibly referring to acts or aspects of protervity; appears in highly specialized or fictional contexts.
examples: The notes discuss various protervities observed in the dataset.
In the novella, protervities drive the plot forward.

senses.definition: A rare or archaic noun suggesting persistence of early traits or tendencies; used in specialized or poetic contexts.
examples: The theme explores protervity as a moral constant.
Protervity shapes the narrator's choices.

senses.definition: A statement or action showing strong disagreement or objection to something; a public display or demonstration.
examples: People organized a protest against the new policy.
The protest lasted several hours in the square.

senses.definition: To express strong disagreement or objection publicly or formally.
examples: Workers protested the decision with banners.
Citizens protested the plan outside city hall.

senses.definition: Able to be protested or worthy of objection; capable of being challenged publicly.
examples: The policy is protestable and merits discussion.
Many aspects of the decision are protestable.

senses.definition: A rare or historical term referring to the quality or act of protesting; used in archaic or literary contexts.
examples: The text speaks of protestancy in the civic rites.
Protestancy is rarely used in modern English.

senses.definition: A member of a Christian church that separated from the Roman Catholic Church during the Reformation; part of major world religions.
examples: He attends a Protestant church.
Protestants emphasize scripture and faith.

senses.definition: Relating to Protestants or their beliefs; describing practices or institutions belonging to Protestantism.
examples: Protestant churches follow various denominations.
Protestant theology centers on the authority of Scripture.

senses.definition: Resembling or characteristic of Protestant beliefs or styles; informal or humorous usage.
examples: The sermon had a Protestantish tone.
Her protestantish approach emphasized scripture.

senses.definition: In a Protestantish manner; in a way that resembles Protestant beliefs or styles.
examples: He spoke protestantishly about reform.
The author writes protestantishly in a moralizing tone.

senses.definition: The beliefs, practices, and systems of Protestant churches and movements.
examples: Protestantism includes many denominations.
He studied the history of Protestantism.

senses.definition: To convert or cause to adopt Protestant beliefs or practices; to make something resemble Protestantism.
examples: They sought to Protestantize the revival movement.
Efforts were made to Protestantize the education system.

senses.definition: Resembling Protestant beliefs or practices; similar in style or principle.
examples: The reform movement took on a protestantlike zeal.
She spoke in a protestantlike, scripture-centered manner.

senses.definition: In a Protestant manner or with Protestant emphasis.
examples: He argued protestantly about the legitimacy of reform.
She cited the scripture protestantly in her defense.

senses.definition: People who belong to Protestant churches or movements.
examples: The region has a large population of Protestants.
Protestants and Catholics attended the ceremony.
